title: hieracium bifidum subsp. kemense
intro: Hieracium bifidum subsp. kemense, also known as Kem's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of the Hieracium bifidum plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium bifidum subsp. kemense include its ability to reduce inflammation and improve immune function, as well as its potential to help with wound healing and the treatment of certain infections. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
